subject: Physics
title: James Clerk Maxwell: Light Is Electromagnetic
description: A fast breakdown of James Clerk Maxwell: Light Is Electromagnetic for builders.

hook.text: Maxwell unified electricity, magnetism, and optics with four equations. Einstein kept his photo on his wall.

buildup.text: By the 1860s, Faraday's experiments and Ampère's math described electricity and magnetism separately.

discovery.text: Maxwell combined them and found that electromagnetic disturbances travel as waves—at exactly the speed of light.

twist.text: He died at 48 from stomach cancer. Had he lived longer, he might have discovered radio waves and more.

climax.text: His equations predicted radio, microwaves, X-rays, and every wireless technology before any were discovered.

punchline.text: Four equations predicted the invisible. Maxwell saw what no one else could.

quiz.question: What did Maxwell predict from his equations before it was experimentally confirmed?
options: That electromagnetic waves travel at the speed of light
